networkAccess.reasoning: Loads a local reconstruction run and its assets, then sends the finished Figma preview back to the QA bridge. Same-machine dev uses http://localhost:8790 (loopback is a Secure-Context exemption, so plain HTTP is allowed). Cross-machine (e.g. Figma Desktop on a Mac, bridge on a headless RTX box) must use HTTPS: Figma's plugin UI runs over HTTPS and Chromium's mixed-content rule blocks HTTPS->HTTP fetches to any non-loopback host, so the bridge is fronted by `tailscale serve` which provides a real cert for the *.ts.net MagicDNS name. IP-literal hosts (127.0.0.1, 100.x) are rejected by Figma's manifest validator and are not trustworthy for HTTPS, so the *.ts.net hostname must be used.
